## v0.0.1

Initial release of the @e412/rnui component library.

### Highlights

- **60+ components** built on [Base UI](https://base-ui.com/) and [shadcn/ui](https://ui.shadcn.com/) patterns
- **Theme system** with `@e412/rnui-themes` -- CSS custom properties in oklch, light/dark mode, Tailwind v4 integration
- **ECharts integration** -- BarChart, LineChart, AreaChart, PieChart, RadarChart, ScatterChart with automatic theme syncing
- **chartConfig() builder** -- fluent API for composing chart options
- **DataGrid** -- full-featured data table with sorting, filtering, pagination, column visibility, drag-and-drop columns/rows, and virtualization
- **DateSelector** -- date selection with calendar integration
- **Filters** -- configurable filter controls
- **Sortable** -- drag-and-drop sortable lists
- **Form integration** -- react-hook-form compatible Form components
- **Tree-shakeable** -- import only what you use, all dependencies externalized

## Installation

Install the React components and themes packages:

```bash
npm install @e412/rnui-react @e412/rnui-themes
```

Or with pnpm:

```bash
pnpm add @e412/rnui-react @e412/rnui-themes
```

### Charts (Optional)

If you plan to use chart components, install ECharts as a peer dependency:

```bash
npm install echarts
```

## CSS Setup

Import the theme CSS in your app's global stylesheet:

```css
@import '@e412/rnui-themes';
@import 'tw-animate-css';
```

## Tailwind v4 Integration

The theme CSS includes a `@theme inline` block that automatically registers all design tokens with Tailwind v4. Just importing `@e412/rnui-themes` gives you utilities like `bg-primary`, `text-muted-foreground`, etc.

Add the library to your Tailwind content sources:

```css
@source "../node_modules/@e412/rnui-react/dist";
```

## Dark Mode

Toggle dark mode by adding the `dark` class to your `<html>` element:

```html
<html class="dark">
```

The theme automatically switches all color tokens.

## Import

```tsx
import { AreaChart } from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

```tsx
<AreaChart
  categories={['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr', 'May']}
  series={[
    { name: 'Revenue', data: [120, 200, 150, 80, 210] },
  ]}
  height={350}
/>
```

## Examples

### Multi-Series with Gradient

```tsx

  categories={['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr', 'May', 'Jun']}

    { name: 'Desktop', data: [120, 200, 150, 80, 210, 180] },

/>
```

### Stacked

### Without Gradient

### Smooth Curves

```tsx

  categories={['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr']}
  series={[
```

A fluent builder API for creating ECharts options with shadcn-compatible defaults.

## Import

```tsx
import { chartConfig } from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

The `chartConfig()` function returns a `ChartConfigBuilder` instance. Chain methods to define series, configure axes, and set options, then call `.build()` to produce the final ECharts option object.

```tsx
import { EChart, chartConfig } from '@e412/rnui-react'

const option = chartConfig()
  .bar({ data: [120, 200, 150], categories: ['A', 'B', 'C'] })
  .tooltip()
  .build()

<EChart option={option} />
```

## Examples

### Multi-Series Line Chart

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .line({ data: [120, 200, 150], categories: ['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar'], name: 'Revenue', smooth: true })
  .line({ data: [80, 120, 100], name: 'Expenses', smooth: true })
  .legend()
  .tooltip()
  .build()
```

### Donut Chart

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .pie({
    data: [
      { name: 'Desktop', value: 65 },
      { name: 'Mobile', value: 30 },
      { name: 'Tablet', value: 5 },
    ],
    donut: true,
  })
  .tooltip('item')
  .legend()
  .build()
```

### Stacked Bar Chart

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .bar({ data: [120, 200, 150], categories: ['Q1', 'Q2', 'Q3'], name: 'Product A', stack: 'total' })
  .bar({ data: [80, 120, 100], name: 'Product B', stack: 'total' })
  .legend()
  .tooltip()
  .build()
```

### Horizontal Bars

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .bar({ data: [420, 310, 180], categories: ['React', 'Vue', 'Angular'], horizontal: true })
  .tooltip()
  .build()
```

### Line with Area Fill

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .line({
    data: [120, 200, 150, 80, 210],
    categories: ['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr', 'May'],
    name: 'Revenue',
    smooth: true,
    area: true,
    areaOpacity: 0.2,
  })
  .tooltip()
  .build()
```

### Scatter Plot

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .scatter({ data: [[10, 8], [8, 7], [13, 8], [9, 9]], name: 'Group A' })
  .scatter({ data: [[6, 4], [4, 3], [12, 10]], name: 'Group B' })
  .legend()
  .tooltip('item')
  .build()
```

### Radar Chart

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .radar({
    indicators: [
      { name: 'Speed', max: 100 },
      { name: 'Reliability', max: 100 },
      { name: 'Cost', max: 100 },
    ],
    series: [
      { name: 'Product A', value: [90, 70, 60] },
      { name: 'Product B', value: [60, 85, 90] },
    ],
  })
  .legend()
  .tooltip('item')
  .build()
```

### Gauge Chart

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .gauge({ value: 72, name: 'Score', min: 0, max: 100 })
  .build()
```

### With Data Zoom

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .bar({ data: longDataArray, categories: longCategoryArray })
  .dataZoom(0, 50)
  .tooltip()
  .build()
```

### Custom Colors and Grid

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .bar({ data: [120, 200, 150], categories: ['A', 'B', 'C'] })
  .colors(['#3b82f6', '#8b5cf6', '#ec4899'])
  .grid({ left: 20, right: 20, top: 30, bottom: 20 })
  .tooltip()
  .build()
```

### Raw Option Merge

For anything not covered by the helper methods, use `.raw()` to merge arbitrary ECharts options:

```tsx
const option = chartConfig()
  .bar({ data: [120, 200, 150], categories: ['A', 'B', 'C'] })
  .raw({ animation: false, backgroundColor: '#fafafa' })
  .build()
```

## API Reference

### Series Methods

| Method | Arguments | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `.bar(config)` | `{ data, name?, categories?, stack?, horizontal?, borderRadius?, barWidth? }` | Add a bar series |
| `.line(config)` | `{ data, name?, categories?, smooth?, area?, areaOpacity? }` | Add a line series |
| `.pie(config)` | `{ data, donut?, innerRadius?, outerRadius? }` | Add a pie/donut series |
| `.scatter(config)` | `{ data, name?, symbolSize? }` | Add a scatter series |
| `.radar(config)` | `{ indicators, series }` | Add a radar chart |
| `.gauge(config)` | `{ value, name?, min?, max? }` | Add a gauge chart |

### Configuration Methods

| Method | Arguments | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `.title(text, subtitle?)` | `string, string?` | Set chart title (ECharts-rendered) |
| `.legend(position?)` | `'top' \| 'bottom' \| 'left' \| 'right'` | Enable legend (default: `'bottom'`) |
| `.tooltip(trigger?, formatter?)` | `'axis' \| 'item'`, `string \| Function` | Enable tooltip (default trigger: `'axis'`) |
| `.dataZoom(start?, end?)` | `number, number` | Enable data zoom slider (default: `0`-`100`) |
| `.grid(margins)` | `{ left?, right?, top?, bottom? }` | Set custom grid margins |
| `.colors(palette)` | `string[]` | Set custom color palette |
| `.raw(opts)` | `Partial<EChartsOption>` | Merge arbitrary ECharts options |
| `.build()` | â€” | Build the final `EChartsOption` object |

Data visualization components powered by Apache ECharts with automatic shadcn theme integration.

## Overview

The chart components wrap [Apache ECharts](https://echarts.apache.org/) with React bindings and automatic shadcn/ui theme integration. Charts automatically adapt to light and dark mode, use your design system's color tokens (`--chart-1` through `--chart-5`), and include sensible defaults via the built-in preset.

## Installation

ECharts is a peer dependency. Install it alongside the component library:

```bash
npm install echarts
```

Or with pnpm:

```bash
pnpm add echarts
```

## Import

```tsx
import { EChart } from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Base Component

The `EChart` component manages the full ECharts instance lifecycle -- initialization, option updates, theme switching, resize handling, and cleanup.

```tsx
import { EChart } from '@e412/rnui-react'

<EChart
  option={{
    xAxis: { type: 'category', data: ['Mon', 'Tue', 'Wed'] },
    yAxis: { type: 'value' },
    series: [{ type: 'bar', data: [120, 200, 150] }],
  }}
  height={350}
/>
```

## Theme Integration

Charts automatically detect whether your app is in light or dark mode by watching for the `.dark` class on `<html>`. The built-in `shadcn-light` and `shadcn-dark` themes map your CSS custom properties (`--chart-1` through `--chart-5`, `--background`, `--foreground`, `--border`, etc.) to ECharts theme values.

You can also pass a specific theme:

```tsx
<EChart option={option} theme="dark" />
<EChart option={option} theme="light" />
```

## Preset

The `preset` prop (enabled by default) applies minimal, opinionated defaults that match shadcn styling -- clean axis lines, subtle grid lines, proper spacing, and tooltip styling. Disable it for raw ECharts behavior:

```tsx
<EChart option={option} preset={false} />
```

## Available Charts

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| [BarChart](/charts/bar-chart) | Vertical and horizontal bar charts |
| [LineChart](/charts/line-chart) | Line charts with optional smoothing |
| [AreaChart](/charts/area-chart) | Area charts with gradient fills |
| [PieChart](/charts/pie-chart) | Pie and donut charts |
| [RadarChart](/charts/radar-chart) | Radar/spider charts |
| [ScatterChart](/charts/scatter-chart) | Scatter plot charts |

## Configuration

Use the [chartConfig()](/charts/chart-config) builder API to programmatically create chart options with a fluent interface.

## Props (EChart)

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `option` | `EChartsCoreOption` | â€” | ECharts option object |
| `width` | `number \| string` | `'100%'` | Chart width |
| `height` | `number \| string` | `350` | Chart height |
| `theme` | `string` | auto | Theme name (`'light'`, `'dark'`, or custom) |
| `preset` | `boolean` | `true` | Apply shadcn-style minimal defaults |
| `renderer` | `'canvas' \| 'svg'` | `'canvas'` | Rendering engine |
| `loading` | `boolean` | `false` | Show loading indicator |
| `loadingOption` | `LoadingOption` | â€” | Loading indicator options |
| `onEvents` | `Record<string, Function>` | â€” | ECharts event handlers |
| `autoResize` | `boolean` | `true` | Auto-resize on container change |
| `animateOnMount` | `boolean` | `true` | Run mount animation sequence |
| `animateOnMountDelayMs` | `number` | `16` | Delay before mount animation |
| `notMerge` | `boolean` | `false` | Replace option instead of merging |
| `lazyUpdate` | `boolean` | `false` | Lazy update mode |
| `className` | `string` | â€” | Additional CSS classes |
| `style` | `CSSProperties` | â€” | Inline styles |

## Ref

Access the ECharts instance via ref:

```tsx
import { useRef } from 'react'
import { EChart, type ChartRef } from '@e412/rnui-react'

function MyChart() {
  const chartRef = useRef<ChartRef>(null)

  return (
    <EChart
      ref={chartRef}
      option={option}
    />
  )
}

// chartRef.current.getEchartsInstance() â€” get raw ECharts instance
// chartRef.current.resize({ width, height }) â€” manually resize
```

Options | Option | Type | Default | Description | |--------|------|---------|-------------| | `maxFiles` | `number` | `Infinity` | Maximum number of files allowed | | `maxSize` | `number` | `Infinity` | Maximum file size in bytes | | `accept` | `string` | `'*'` | Accepted file types (MIME types or extensions, comma-separated) | | `multiple` | `boolean` | `false` | Allow multiple file selection | | `initialFiles` | `FileMetadata[]` | `[]` | Pre-populated files | | `onFilesChange` | `(files: FileWithPreview[]) => void` | â€” | Called when the file list changes | | `onFilesAdded` | `(files: FileWithPreview[]) => void` | â€” | Called when new files are added | | `onError` | `(errors: string[]) => void` | â€” | Called when validation errors occur | ## Return Value The hook returns a tuple of `[state, actions]`. ### State | Property | Type | Description | |----------|------|-------------| | `files` | `FileWithPreview[]` | Current file list with preview URLs | | `isDragging` | `boolean` | Whether a drag operation is active over the drop zone | | `errors` | `string[]` | Current validation error messages | ### Actions | Method | Type | Description | |--------|------|-------------| | `addFiles` | `(files: FileList \| File[]) => void` | Add files programmatically | | `removeFile` | `(id: string) => void` | Remove a file by ID | | `clearFiles` | `() => void` | Remove all files | | `clearErrors` | `() => void` | Clear all error messages | | `handleDragEnter` | `DragEventHandler` | Attach to drop zone `onDragEnter` | | `handleDragLeave` | `DragEventHandler` | Attach to drop zone `onDragLeave` | | `handleDragOver` | `DragEventHandler` | Attach to drop zone `onDragOver` | | `handleDrop` | `DragEventHandler` | Attach to drop zone `onDrop` | | `handleFileChange` | `ChangeEventHandler` | Attach to file input `onChange` | | `openFileDialog` | `() => void` | Open the native file picker | | `getInputProps` | `(props?) => InputProps` | Get props to spread on a hidden `` | ## Types ```tsx type FileMetadata = { name: string size: number type: string url: string id: string } type FileWithPreview = { file: File | FileMetadata id: string preview?: string } ``` ## Utility The `formatBytes` helper is also exported: ```tsx import { formatBytes } from '@e412/rnui-react' formatBytes(1024) // "1KB" formatBytes(1048576) // "1MB" formatBytes(0) // "0 Bytes" ``` ## {isMobile ? ( ) : ( )}

Feature-rich data grid built on TanStack Table with sorting, filtering, pagination, column resizing, drag-and-drop columns/rows, and virtual scrolling.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @e412/rnui-react @e412/rnui-themes
```

## Import

```tsx
import {
  DataGrid,
  DataGridContainer,
  useDataGrid,
} from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

Additional sub-components are available for specific features:

```tsx
import { DataGridTable } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { DataGridTableVirtual } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { DataGridTableDnd } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { DataGridTableDndRows } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { DataGridPagination } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { DataGridColumnVisibility } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { DataGridColumnHeader } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { DataGridColumnFilter } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { DataGridScrollArea } from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

```tsx
import {
  useReactTable,
  getCoreRowModel,
  createColumnHelper,
} from '@tanstack/react-table'

const columnHelper = createColumnHelper<User>()

const columns = [
  columnHelper.accessor('name', { header: 'Name' }),
  columnHelper.accessor('email', { header: 'Email' }),
  columnHelper.accessor('role', { header: 'Role' }),
]

function UsersTable() {
  const table = useReactTable({
    data,
    columns,
    getCoreRowModel: getCoreRowModel(),
  })

  return (
    <DataGrid table={table} recordCount={data.length}>
      <DataGridContainer>
        <DataGridTable />
      </DataGridContainer>
    </DataGrid>
  )
}
```

```tsx
})

<DataGrid table={table} recordCount={data.length}>
  <DataGridContainer>
    <DataGridTable />
  </DataGridContainer>
</DataGrid>
```

A multi-period date selector supporting day, month, quarter, half-year, and year selection with filter operators.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @e412/rnui-react @e412/rnui-themes
```

## Import

```tsx
import {
  DateSelector,
  formatDateValue,
  useDateSelector,
} from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

```tsx
import { DateSelector } from '@e412/rnui-react'

export function DateSelectorDemo() {
  const [value, setValue] = useState<DateSelectorValue | undefined>()

  return <DateSelector value={value} onChange={setValue} />
}
```

## Examples

### Default (Day Picker)

```tsx
<DateSelector value={value} onChange={setValue} />
```

### Month Period

```tsx
<DateSelector
  value={value}
  onChange={setValue}
  defaultPeriodType="month"
/>
```

### Quarter and Year Only

```tsx
<DateSelector
  value={value}
  onChange={setValue}
  periodTypes={['quarter', 'year']}
  defaultPeriodType="quarter"
/>
```

### Range Selection Disabled

```tsx
<DateSelector
  value={value}
  onChange={setValue}
  allowRange={false}
/>
```

### Preset Filter Mode

```tsx
<DateSelector
  value={value}
  onChange={setValue}
  presetMode="between"
/>
```

### Custom Year Range

```tsx
<DateSelector
  value={value}
  onChange={setValue}
  minYear={2020}
  maxYear={2030}
/>
```

### Without Input

```tsx
<DateSelector
  value={value}
  onChange={setValue}
  showInput={false}
/>
```

### Custom Date Format

```tsx
<DateSelector
  value={value}
  onChange={setValue}
  dayDateFormat="dd/MM/yyyy"
/>
```

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `value` | `DateSelectorValue` | - | Controlled value |
| `onChange` | `(value: DateSelectorValue) => void` | - | Change handler |
| `allowRange` | `boolean` | `true` | Allow range selection (between operator) |
| `periodTypes` | `DateSelectorPeriodType[]` | All periods | Which period tabs to show |
| `defaultPeriodType` | `"day" \| "month" \| "quarter" \| "half-year" \| "year"` | `"day"` | Initial period type |
| `defaultFilterType` | `"is" \| "before" \| "after" \| "between"` | `"is"` | Initial filter operator |
| `presetMode` | `DateSelectorFilterType` | - | Lock to a specific filter operator |
| `showInput` | `boolean` | `true` | Show the date text input |
| `showTwoMonths` | `boolean` | `true` | Show two calendar months side by side |
| `label` | `string` | - | Label text above the selector |
| `className` | `string` | - | Additional CSS class |
| `yearRange` | `number` | `10` | Number of years to display |
| `minYear` | `number` | `2015` | Earliest selectable year |
| `maxYear` | `number` | `2026` | Latest selectable year |
| `i18n` | `Partial<DateSelectorI18nConfig>` | English defaults | Internationalization overrides |

| `weekStartsOn` | `0 \| 1 \| 2 \| 3 \| 4 \| 5 \| 6` | - | First day of the week (0 = Sunday) |

### Command Dialog

Use `CommandDialog` to render the command palette in a centered modal dialog.

```tsx
const [open, setOpen] = React.useState(false)

React.useEffect(() => {
  const down = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
    if (e.key === 'k' && (e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey)) {
      e.preventDefault()
      setOpen((open) => !open)
    }
  }
  document.addEventListener('keydown', down)
  return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', down)
}, [])

<CommandDialog open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
  <Command>
    <CommandInput placeholder="Type a command or search..." />
    <CommandList>
      <CommandEmpty>No results found.</CommandEmpty>
      <CommandGroup heading="Actions">
        <CommandItem>New File</CommandItem>
        <CommandItem>New Folder</CommandItem>
      </CommandGroup>
      <CommandSeparator />
      <CommandGroup heading="Navigation">
        <CommandItem>Home</CommandItem>
        <CommandItem>Settings</CommandItem>
      </CommandGroup>
    </CommandList>
  </Command>
</CommandDialog>
```

## Import

## Usage

```tsx
import { Filters, createFilter, type Filter, type FilterFieldConfig } from '@e412/rnui-react'

const fields: FilterFieldConfig[] = [
  {
    key: 'status',
    label: 'Status',
    type: 'select',
    options: [
      { value: 'active', label: 'Active' },
      { value: 'inactive', label: 'Inactive' },
    ],
  },
  {
    key: 'name',
    label: 'Name',
    type: 'text',
  },

  )
}
```

A form wrapper built on react-hook-form with accessible labels, descriptions, and validation messages.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @e412/rnui-react @e412/rnui-themes
```

## Import

```tsx
import {
  Form,
  FormField,
  FormItem,
  FormLabel,
  FormControl,
  FormDescription,
  FormMessage,
  useFormField,
} from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

```tsx
import { useForm } from 'react-hook-form'
import { Form, FormField, FormItem, FormLabel, FormControl, FormMessage } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { Input } from '@e412/rnui-react'

export function FormDemo() {
  const form = useForm({ defaultValues: { email: '' } })

  return (
    <Form {...form}>
      <form onSubmit={form.handleSubmit((data) => console.log(data))}>
        <FormField
          control={form.control}
          name="email"
          render={({ field }) => (
            <FormItem>
              <FormLabel>Email</FormLabel>
              <FormControl>
                <Input placeholder="you@example.com" {...field} />
              </FormControl>
              <FormMessage />
            </FormItem>
          )}
        />
      </form>
    </Form>
  )
}
```

A dropdown select component for choosing a value from a list of options.


## Import

```tsx
import {
  Select,
  SelectContent,
  SelectGroup,
  SelectItem,
  SelectLabel,
  SelectSeparator,
  SelectTrigger,
  SelectValue,
} from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

```tsx
<Select defaultValue="apple">
  <SelectTrigger>
    <SelectValue placeholder="Select a fruit" />
  </SelectTrigger>
  <SelectContent>
    <SelectGroup>
      <SelectLabel>Fruits</SelectLabel>
      <SelectItem value="apple">Apple</SelectItem>
      <SelectItem value="banana">Banana</SelectItem>
      <SelectItem value="orange">Orange</SelectItem>

</Select>
```

Toast notification system using the Sonner library, themed to match your design system.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @e412/rnui-react @e412/rnui-themes
```

## Import

```tsx
import { Toaster } from '@e412/rnui-react'
import { toast } from 'sonner'
```

## Usage

Add the `Toaster` component to your root layout, then call `toast()` from anywhere in your app.

### Setup

```tsx
// In your root layout (e.g., layout.tsx)
import { Toaster } from '@e412/rnui-react'

export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <html>
      <body>
        {children}
        <Toaster />
      </body>
    </html>
  )
}
```

### Triggering Toasts

```tsx
import { toast } from 'sonner'

<Button onClick={() => toast("Event has been created")}>
  Show Toast
</Button>
```

## Examples

### Success

```tsx
<Button
  onClick={() => toast.success("Changes saved successfully")}
>
  Save
</Button>
```

### Error

```tsx
<Button
  variant="destructive"
  onClick={() => toast.error("Something went wrong")}
>
  Delete
</Button>
```

### Warning

```tsx
<Button
  onClick={() => toast.warning("Please check your input")}
>
  Validate
</Button>
```

### Info

```tsx
<Button
  onClick={() => toast.info("A new version is available")}
>
  Check Update
</Button>
```

### With Description

```tsx
<Button
  onClick={() =>
    toast("Event Created", {
      description: "Your event has been scheduled for tomorrow at 10am.",
    })
  }
>
  Create Event
</Button>
```

### With Action

```tsx
<Button
  onClick={() =>
    toast("File deleted", {
      action: {
        label: "Undo",
        onClick: () => console.log("Undo delete"),
      },
    })
  }
>
  Delete File
</Button>
```

### Loading / Promise

```tsx
<Button
  onClick={() =>
    toast.promise(
      fetch("/api/data").then((res) => res.json()),
      {
        loading: "Loading data...",
        success: "Data loaded!",
        error: "Failed to load data",
      }
    )
  }
>
  Fetch Data
</Button>
```

### Custom Position

```tsx
// Place the Toaster at the top-center
<Toaster position="top-center" />
```

## Props

### Toaster

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `position` | `"top-left" \| "top-center" \| "top-right" \| "bottom-left" \| "bottom-center" \| "bottom-right"` | `"bottom-right"` | Toast position on screen |
| `expand` | `boolean` | `false` | Expand toasts by default |
| `richColors` | `boolean` | `false` | Use rich colors for toast types |
| `closeButton` | `boolean` | `false` | Show close button on toasts |
| `duration` | `number` | `4000` | Auto-dismiss duration in ms |
| `theme` | `"light" \| "dark" \| "system"` | `"system"` | Theme (auto-detected from next-themes) |

### toast() Function

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `toast(message)` | Show a default toast |
| `toast.success(message)` | Show a success toast |
| `toast.error(message)` | Show an error toast |
| `toast.warning(message)` | Show a warning toast |
| `toast.info(message)` | Show an info toast |
| `toast.loading(message)` | Show a loading toast |
| `toast.promise(promise, options)` | Show loading/success/error based on promise |
| `toast.dismiss(id?)` | Dismiss a specific or all toasts |

A tree view component powered by headless-tree for rendering hierarchical data with expand/collapse, selection, drag-and-drop, and search support.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @e412/rnui-react @e412/rnui-themes @headless-tree/core @headless-tree/react
```

## Import

```tsx
import { Tree, TreeItem, TreeItemLabel, TreeDragLine } from '@e412/rnui-react'
```

## Usage

```tsx
import { useTree } from '@headless-tree/react'

const items = {
  root: { name: "Root", children: ["src", "public"] },
  src: { name: "src", children: ["app", "lib"] },
  public: { name: "public", children: ["favicon"] },
  app: { name: "app.tsx" },
  lib: { name: "lib.ts" },
  favicon: { name: "favicon.ico" },
}

function MyTree() {
  const tree = useTree({
    rootItemId: "root",
    getItemName: (item) => item.name,
    isItemFolder: (item) => !!item.children,
    dataLoader: {
      getItem: (id) => items[id],
      getChildren: (id) => items[id].children ?? [],
    },
  })

  return (
    <Tree tree={tree}>
      {tree.getItems().map((item) => (
        <TreeItem key={item.getId()} item={item}>
          <TreeItemLabel />
        </TreeItem>
      ))}
    </Tree>
  )
}
```

## Examples

### Chevron Toggle Icons

```tsx
<Tree tree={tree} toggleIconType="chevron">
  {tree.getItems().map((item) => (
    <TreeItem key={item.getId()} item={item}>
      <TreeItemLabel />
    </TreeItem>
  ))}
</Tree>
```

### Plus/Minus Toggle Icons

```tsx
<Tree tree={tree} toggleIconType="plus-minus">
  {tree.getItems().map((item) => (
    <TreeItem key={item.getId()} item={item}>
      <TreeItemLabel />
    </TreeItem>
  ))}
</Tree>
```

### Custom Indent

```tsx
<Tree tree={tree} indent={32}>
  {tree.getItems().map((item) => (
    <TreeItem key={item.getId()} item={item}>
      <TreeItemLabel />
    </TreeItem>
  ))}
</Tree>
```

### With Drag Line

```tsx
<Tree tree={tree}>
  {tree.getItems().map((item) => (
    <TreeItem key={item.getId()} item={item}>
      <TreeItemLabel />
    </TreeItem>
  ))}
  <TreeDragLine />
</Tree>
```

## Props

### Tree

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `tree` | `TreeInstance` | -- | Tree instance from `useTree()` (headless-tree). |
| `indent` | `number` | `20` | Pixel indentation per nesting level. |
| `toggleIconType` | `"chevron" \| "plus-minus"` | `"chevron"` | Style of the expand/collapse icon for folder items. |
